arts.ttl: Farewell to the normal one: Carsley puts England’s players first as he signs off
arts.sum: Interim manager knows praise and pillory are part of the job and will hand on to Thomas Tuchel having made bold calls The contradiction with Lee Carsley is that he is a normal man who feels mysterious in a football context. Managers at the highest level are supposed to be egomaniacs, but Carsley has almost seemed allergic to taking any credit for tactical triumphs during his time as England’s interim head coach. The 50-year-old has never wanted to be the story. He prefers the focus to be on his players. But managing England is unlike anything else. The scrutiny is intense, and not only when it comes to team selection. A pile-on lurks behind every corner. Carsley learned that the hard way before facing the Republic of Ireland in his first game two months ago, sparking an absurd reaction with his refusal to sing the national anthem. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Harry Kane confident Tuchel ‘energy’ can preserve England’s fragile culture
arts.sum: Captain reiterates need to maintain Southgate’s inclusive environment as he gets ready to compete for place in team For Harry Kane, it has been a time for straight-talking. The England captain did row back slightly on his comments from Wednesday – the bombshell ones when he wondered whether the unprecedented number of withdrawals from the squad for this window was the result of players “taking advantage” of a tough period in the season. At one point, as Kane took a break from the preparations for Sunday’s Nations League tie against Republic of Ireland at Wembley – the final game of Lee Carsley’s interim tenure – he expressed a surprise at the wall-to-wall coverage his words had driven. Memo to Harry: Seriously? Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Lee Carsley ready to return to Starbucks in peace after England stint comes to an end
arts.sum: England's interim manager Lee Carsley will take charge of the sixth and final match of his stint when the Republic of Ireland come to Wembley on Sunday in the Nations League

arts.ttl: Harry Kane makes Cristiano Ronaldo claim after admitting "shock" at England decision
arts.sum: Harry Kane is already England's all-time record goalscorer but the Bayern Munich is determined not to hang up his goalscoring shoes just yet as he aims to emulate Cristiano Ronaldo's longevity

arts.ttl: Harry Kane predicts Thomas Tuchel 'confrontation' having played under new England boss
arts.sum: Thomas Tuchel will officially take charge of the Three Lions on January 1, with interim coach Lee Carsley set to step down after Sunday's Nations League clash with the Republic of Ireland

arts.ttl: Interim boss Lee Carsley hints he would consider applying for England job as Thomas Tuchel replacement
arts.sum: Carsley believes the interim coaching staff have proved “we can do it”

arts.ttl: England prepare for Republic of Ireland in Carsley’s final game
arts.sum: Hear from Harry Kane and Lee Carsley ahead of England vs Republic of Ireland at Wembley.

arts.ttl: Ireland primed for London battle as pressure on England
arts.sum: "Of course, we know what kind of threat England gives us and if we are not ready from the first minute, they will punish us," said Hallgrimsson, when asked how vital it was that his team are immediately comfortable, both tactically and in formation. "We go like all our games to try to win, but it is of course a little relief knowing we’re not playing for a position, so I think the pressure on England in this game, they need to win game to win the group.

arts.ttl: Heimir Hallgrimsson: England want to win World Cup, Ireland just to get there
arts.sum: The two sides meet in the Nations League at Wembley on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Harry Kane makes worried England admission after ‘surprise’ at big-name dropout row
arts.sum: Harry Kane has taken aim at some of his England teammates choosing not to join up with the current Three Lions squad for clashes against Greece and the Republic of Ireland
